behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: A mock embedding API could always return the correct dimensionality, hiding real API failures where the actual service changed its output shape or version. Calling the real embedding service (or a test double enforcing the service contract) is the minimum to catch dimension mismatches between code and production.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the embedding service with a test query and verify the returned vector has dimensionality equal to the configured vector database index dimension (e.g., 1536 for OpenAI). Reject the query if the service returns a different dimension.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test must call the real embedding service to catch real-world API contract violations; mocking would always succeed.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: Mocking the vector DB query would allow you to pre-order results or guarantee they come back sorted, masking real ordering failures. A real vector DB instance (or test double that doesn't enforce pre-ordering) is required to verify the code handles and sorts unordered results from the database.
behaviors.test_description: Insert test vectors into the vector database with known similarity scores to a query vector (e.g., 0.95, 0.70, 0.85), execute a similarity search, and verify returned results are ordered by descending score (0.95 must appear before 0.70).
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires a real vector database instance to verify ordering is not just incidental to mocking; confirms Integration level.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Mocking an LLM would return whatever output you specify, completely hiding semantic failures. A real LLM call is required to verify it actually understands nuanced intent and reorders results meaningfully; Integration testing of error handling alone would not catch incorrect re-ranking.
behaviors.test_description: Provide the LLM re-ranker with 5 similarly-scored documents and a query with subtle disambiguating context. Verify the LLM output order differs from the input order in at least some cases; if the LLM returns the input order unchanged, the test fails.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test calls a real LLM and checks semantic behavior; mocking would trivially pass, confirming Agentic level is required.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: Unit tests of filtering logic alone ('if score < threshold, exclude') would not catch if the filter is never applied in the production code path or if the threshold is misconfigured. Integration testing with a real vector DB that returns only low-relevance results verifies the end-to-end filtering behavior and application.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a search query against the vector database using a test vector that yields only results with similarity scores below the relevance threshold (e.g., 0.3 when threshold is 0.5). Verify the API returns an empty result set, not low-relevance candidates.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test must use a real vector database to verify filtering is actually applied end-to-end; Unit tests of the filtering function alone would not catch application failures.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: Unit and Integration tests with mocked external services would hide real network latency, embedding service timeouts, vector DB query time, and LLM inference latency. Running the full stack with real external services (or near-production replicas with realistic latency) is required to measure true end-to-end latency.
behaviors.test_description: Execute 100 end-to-end queries against real or near-production embedding service, vector database, and LLM re-ranker. Measure wall-clock latency for each query. Calculate and verify median latency is below 2 seconds; fail if median exceeds 2 seconds.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The test requires real external services with actual network and inference latencies; mocking would hide real performance bottlenecks, confirming System level is required.
